title: Chain-agnostic nullifier registry with cross-chain replay protection

artifacts.description: Add chainId as a public input; update nullifierHash derivation to Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId)
Add chainId as a public input; update nullifierHash derivation to Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId)
Shared template wrapping Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId) so both circuits import a single nullifier derivation
On-chain nullifier set that validates msg.chainId (block.chainid) matches the public input chainId before marking a nullifier as spent
Updated Groth16 verifier stub regenerated from new circuit; enforces chainId public input matches block.chainid
Updated Groth16 verifier stub regenerated from new circuit; enforces chainId public input matches block.chainid
Formal specification of the nullifier domain-separation scheme, threat model (cross-chain replay), and migration path from Poseidon2 to Poseidon3
Update client-side nullifier computation helper to accept chainId parameter and call poseidon3([scope, secret, chainId])
Pass chainId as witness input when generating proofs for HumanUniqueness and AgentPolicy circuits
Circom unit tests: same (scope, secret) on different chainIds must produce distinct nullifiers; cross-chain replay attempt must fail
Solidity integration tests: verify proof with correct chainId succeeds; mismatched chainId reverts; nullifier double-spend on same chain reverts
Migration guide for integrators: describes the breaking circuit change, how to re-derive nullifiers, and re-registration requirements for existing users

steps: Create circuits/lib/NullifierLib.circom with a Poseidon3(scope, secret, chainId) template to centralize nullifier derivation
Update HumanUniqueness.circom: add signal input chainId (public), replace Poseidon2 nullifier call with NullifierLib template
Update AgentPolicy.circom: same chainId public input addition and NullifierLib substitution
Recompile both circuits with snarkjs/circom2, regenerate R1CS, zkey, and Verifier.sol artifacts
Update NullifierRegistry.sol to assert that the chainId public input equals block.chainid before spending a nullifier
Regenerate HumanUniquenessVerifier.sol and AgentPolicyVerifier.sol from new circuit verification keys
Update sdk/src/nullifier.ts to expose computeNullifier(scope, secret, chainId) using poseidon3
Update sdk/src/prover.ts to include chainId in the witness inputs for both circuits
Write circuit unit tests covering same-secret cross-chain nullifier distinctness and replay rejection
Write Solidity integration tests covering valid proof acceptance, chainId mismatch revert, and double-spend revert
Write specs/nullifier-chain-binding.md documenting threat model, domain separation rationale, and v1→v2 migration path
Run full test suite across circuits, contracts, and SDK; verify no regressions
Tag circuit artifacts with a v2 semver bump and publish updated SDK
dependencies: poseidon-solidity or equivalent on-chain Poseidon3 implementation
Hardhat >= 2.19 with circom plugin for integrated circuit tests
Existing HumanUniqueness.circom and AgentPolicy.circom source files
Existing NullifierRegistry.sol deployment addresses (for migration coordination)
